text: This is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: In December, doctors in Mumbai, India reported about a group of patients with what they called totally drug -resistant tuberculosis.
zh: 去年12月,印度孟买的医生报告了一组患者,称他们患有“完全耐药性”肺结核。

text: Indian health officials have been investigating these cases.
zh: 印度卫生官员一直在调查这些病例。

text: But there have been reports of untreatable cases of TB in the past.
zh: 但此前也曾有过无法治愈的结核病例报告。

text: Doctors reported 15 patients in Iran in 2009 and 2 patients in Italy in 2007.
zh: 医生曾报告2009年伊朗有15名患者,2007年意大利有2名患者。

text: Tuberculosis is a bacterial disease that usually targets the lungs.
zh: 结核病是一种通常侵袭肺部的细菌性疾病。

text: It causes an estimated 5 ,000 deaths each day or about 2 million a year.
zh: 据估计,它每天导致5000人死亡,每年约200万人死亡。

text: Some forms of TB bacteria no longer react to one or more of the antibiotics commonly used to cure the disease.
zh: 某些结核杆菌菌株已不再对常用于治疗该病的一种或多种抗生素产生反应。

text: These are known as drug -resistant strains.
zh: 这些被称为耐药菌株。

text: Some resist even more drugs.
zh: 有些菌株甚至对更多药物产生耐药性。

text: The World Health Organization says 69 countries have reported cases of extensively drug -resistant tuberculosis.
zh: 世界卫生组织表示,已有69个国家报告了广泛耐药性肺结核病例。

text: The WHO says at least 25 ,000 such cases are reported worldwide every year.
zh: 世卫组织称,全球每年至少报告2.5万例此类病例。

text: The agency's director general, Dr. Margaret Chan, is concerned about the spread of drug -resistant TB.
zh: 该机构总干事陈冯富珍博士对耐药性结核病的蔓延感到担忧。

text: She says, call it what you may, a time mom or a powder keg, any way you look at it, this is a potentially explosive situation.
zh: 她说,不管你怎么称呼它——定时炸弹也好,火药桶也罢,无论怎么看,这都是一个潜在的爆炸性局面。

text: Officials say drug -resistant TB has been a growing problem in countries like India and China.
zh: 官员们表示,耐药性结核病在印度和中国等国已成为日益严重的问题。

text: In many cases, doctors misdiagnose patients or give them the wrong treatment or not enough treatment.
zh: 在许多情况下,医生误诊患者,或给予错误的治疗,或治疗不足。

text: Misuse of these antibiotics increases the danger that the bacteria will develop resistance to the drugs.
zh: 滥用这些抗生素增加了细菌产生耐药性的风险。

text: Neeraj Mistry is a public health doctor.
zh: Neeraj Mistry 是一名公共卫生医生。

text: He says surveys show that very few Indian doctors are treating TB patients with the right drugs for the right length of time.
zh: 他说,调查显示,很少有印度医生能以正确的药物和正确的疗程来治疗结核病患者。

text: Another problem is that patients may not take all of their medicine.
zh: 另一个问题是,患者可能没有按规定服完全部药物。

text: The emergence of totally resistant TB is a result of failed public health intervention strategies.
zh: 完全耐药性结核病的出现,是公共卫生干预策略失败的结果。

text: When we deliver ineffective treatment regimens and when we don't have full adherence and compliance to treatment,
zh: 当我们提供无效的治疗方案,而且患者又不能完全遵从和坚持治疗时,

text: it enables the emergence of resistance within the individual.
zh: 这就使耐药性得以在个体体内产生。

text: The WHO says new, stronger TB drugs could be available by the end of this year or early 2013.
zh: 世卫组织表示,更强效的新型结核病药物可能会在今年年底或2013年初问世。

text: Researchers are also working on vaccines to prevent the disease.
zh: 研究人员也在研发预防该疾病的疫苗。

text: Dr. Ann Ginzburg works at the ARIS Global TB Vaccine Foundation.
zh: Ann Ginzburg 博士在阿里斯全球结核病疫苗基金会工作。

text: She says the development process takes a long time, partly because people often do not get sick for years after getting infected with TB.
zh: 她说,研发过程耗时很长,部分原因是人们感染结核病后往往数年都不会发病。

text: be.
zh: 是这样。

text: So when you do a vaccine trial, you have to vaccinate people and watch them for years.
zh: 所以在进行疫苗试验时,你必须给人们接种疫苗,并观察他们长达数年。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Carolyn Presutti.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语,我是Carolyn Presutti。
